author: @philip5940
text: And so zoe from the greek means life, living. ,   and trope means turn , turning. That then is the etymology of zoetrope. For a zoo too the animals are all alive, they ain't stuffed exhibitions .

replies.author: @Derek_Garnham
replies.text: Cheers, I love etymology and had never thought about zoetrope

author: @philip5940
text: I see what jogged Baird's design format for the television device he was inventing/trying to invent. Memory of it is getting fragmented now . For TV watching folk had to watch his TV through an eyehole . There's some tussle between Germany, America, Japan, Britain concerning various aspects of television circuitry and transmission inventions .

author: @dav1dbone
text: Getting into 3d printing myself, definitely worthwhile, replacing broken equipment by designing in tinkercad and bolstering areas that need extra strength, amazing. replies.author: @philip5940
replies.text: I noted the other day that mig 21s with modern equipment of course don't necessarily have an inventory of spare parts but are 3d printed these days. A number of methods available to get a plastic shape to give birth to a metal shape if required.

author: @Derek_Garnham
text: Robert, let me get this straight, You 3D printed a zoetrope but then only used it to view 2D pictures! It's been a latent project of mine to produce a zoetrope with 3D models/scenes in place of the pictures.  If you do it, I won't have to :) which suits me at the time as I'm rather busy.
